title: Robustly Spiced German Pork Stew
description: A hearty German stew featuring pork, mixed spices, and a sour twist, perfect for a cold monsoon day, this dish is also nut-free-friendly and has a bouncy qq texture from the addition of potatoes and carrots.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the pork cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the mixed spice, thyme, and bay leaves, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the apple cider vinegar, chicken broth, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender.
Remove the bay leaves and serve hot.
Let the stew cool, then refrigerate or freeze for later use.
Reheat the stew over low heat, stirring occasionally, until warmed through.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
